name: mini-typescript-hero
description: Sorts and organizes TypeScript/JavaScript imports — Keep your codebase clean with custom import grouping, automatic cleanup, and smart formatting. Small hero. Big cleanup!

commands.title: Mini TS Hero: Organize imports (sort and remove unused)
Mini TS Hero: Organize imports in workspace
commands.description: Organize imports in all TypeScript and JavaScript files (.ts, .tsx, .js, .jsx) in the workspace

commands.title: Mini TS Hero: Organize imports in folder
commands.description: Organize imports in all TypeScript and JavaScript files (.ts, .tsx, .js, .jsx) in the selected folder

commands.title: Mini TS Hero: Check for configuration conflicts
Mini TS Hero: Toggle legacy mode

miniTypescriptHero.imports.insertSpaceBe.description: Defines if there should be a space inside the curly braces of an import statement.

miniTypescriptHero.imports.removeTrailin.description: Defines if a trailing '/index' should be removed from imports.

miniTypescriptHero.imports.insertSemicol.description: Defines if there should be a semicolon at the end of a statement. Fallback setting - respects VSCode 'typescript.format.semicolons' and 'javascript.format.semicolons' first.

miniTypescriptHero.imports.stringQuoteSt.description: Defines if single or double quotes should be used. Fallback setting - respects VSCode 'typescript.preferences.quoteStyle' and 'javascript.preferences.quoteStyle' first.

miniTypescriptHero.imports.multiLineWrap.description: Defines the threshold when an import should be wrapped into a multiline import.

miniTypescriptHero.imports.multiLineTrai.description: Defines if multiline imports contain the last trailing comma.

miniTypescriptHero.imports.organizeOnSav.description: Defines if the imports should be organized on save.

miniTypescriptHero.imports.organizeSorts.description: Defines if the imports are organized by first specifier/alias instead of module path. NOTE: This setting has no effect when disableImportsSorting is true (sorting is disabled) or when legacyMode is true (legacy mode always sorts by library name).

miniTypescriptHero.imports.disableImport.description: Defines if sorting is disabled during organize imports. NOTE: This setting has no effect when legacyMode is true (legacy mode always sorts within groups).
Defines if any imports should be removed at all on an organize imports command.

miniTypescriptHero.imports.mergeImportsF.description: Merge multiple import statements from the same module into a single import. For example, 'import { A } from "./lib"' and 'import { B } from "./lib"' become 'import { A, B } from "./lib"'. This setting is independent from removal behavior.

miniTypescriptHero.imports.excludePatter.markdownDescription:
Additional glob patterns for files to exclude from import organization (workspace and single-file operations). These patterns are added to the built-in defaults: `**/node_modules/**`, `**/dist/**`, `**/build/**`, `**/out/**`, `**/.git/**`, `**/coverage/**`.

Common examples:
- `**/.next/**` (Next.js build output)
- `**/.nuxt/**` (Nuxt build output)
- `**/tmp/**` (temporary files)
- `**/generated/**` (auto-generated code)

**Team collaboration:** When organizing a single file that matches an exclude pattern, a warning is shown to inform the user.

miniTypescriptHero.imports.ignoredFromRe.description: Defines imports (libraries, so the 'from' part), which are not removed during 'organize imports'.

miniTypescriptHero.imports.legacyMode.description: Enable full legacy mode for formatting compatibility with old TypeScript Hero extension (with documented exceptions for crashes and edge cases). When true: replicates old formatting behaviors (within-group sorting bug, preserve blank lines, merge-before-removeTrailingIndex timing, type-only merge). When false: modern best practices (correct sorting, 1 blank line, correct merge timing, type-only separation). Migrated users get 'true' automatically.

miniTypescriptHero.imports.tabSize.markdownDescription:
**Only used when `useOnlyExtensionSettings` is true.** Otherwise, VS Code `editor.tabSize` is always used.

**VS Code always has a value** for `editor.tabSize` (default: 4 spaces). Modern mode overrides this to 2 spaces when VS Code is at its default value.

**Note:** VS Code automatically reads `editor.tabSize` from `.editorconfig` files (if EditorConfig extension is installed). We read the final resolved value, so EditorConfig integration works automatically.

miniTypescriptHero.imports.insertSpaces.markdownDescription:
**Only used when `useOnlyExtensionSettings` is true.** Otherwise, VS Code `editor.insertSpaces` is always used.

**VS Code always has a value** for `editor.insertSpaces` (default: true).

**Note:** VS Code automatically reads `editor.insertSpaces` from `.editorconfig` files (if EditorConfig extension is installed). We read the final resolved value, so EditorConfig integration works automatically.

miniTypescriptHero.imports.useOnlyExtens.markdownDescription:
When enabled, ignores VS Code settings and uses only Mini TypeScript Hero extension settings for all formatting options (quotes, semicolons, indentation).

**Use case:** Enforce consistent import formatting across all team members regardless of their editor configuration.

miniTypescriptHero.imports.blankLinesAft.description: Number of blank lines after the last import before code starts. 'one' = exactly 1 (Google/ESLint/Prettier standard), 'two' = exactly 2, 'preserve' = keep existing blank lines. NOTE: This setting has no effect when legacyMode is true (legacy mode always uses 'preserve' behavior).

anyOf.description: Regex pattern for custom grouping (e.g., '/^@angular/', '/rxjs/', etc.)

miniTypescriptHero.imports.grouping.description: Defines the groups of imports and their order. Possible values are "Modules" (for library imports), "Plains" (for string-only imports), "Workspace" (for local imports), "Remaining" (catch-all) or a regex pattern (enclosed in forward slashes). You can also specify an object with "identifier" and "order" (asc/desc).

scripts.compile: npm run check-types && npm run lint && node esbuild.js
scripts.package: npm run check-types && npm run lint && node esbuild.js --production
scripts.pretest: npm run compile-tests && npm run compile && npm run lint
